history:addresses_calendar_days_client_p.sql: SELECT a.state, cp.gender, m.category, cd.year, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS online_customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN clients c ON op.billing_client_ref = c.client_record JOIN client_profiles cp ON c.current_client_profile_ref = cp.client_profile_record JOIN addresses a ON c.current_address_ref = a.address_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND a.state = {{string}} AND cp.gender = {{string}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY a.state, cp.gender, m.category, cd.year

history:addresses_calendar_days_clients:.sql: SELECT cd.year, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS current_state_customers, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN ship_a.state = {{string}} THEN op.shipping_client_ref END) AS shipping_state_customers, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN bill_a.state = {{string}} THEN op.billing_client_ref END) AS billing_state_customers FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN clients c ON op.billing_client_ref = c.client_record JOIN addresses current_a ON c.current_address_ref = current_a.address_record LEFT JOIN addresses ship_a ON op.shipping_address_ref = ship_a.address_record LEFT JOIN addresses bill_a ON op.billing_address_ref = bill_a.address_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND current_a.state = {{string}} GROUP BY cd.year
history:addresses_calendar_days_clients:.template: addresses + calendar_days + clients + online_purchases pattern using addresses.address_record, addresses.state, calendar_days.calendar_day_record, calendar_days.year

history:addresses_clients_household_prof.sql: SELECT a.state, ir.lower_bound, ir.upper_bound, count(DISTINCT c.client_record) AS clients FROM clients c JOIN addresses a ON c.current_address_ref = a.address_record JOIN household_profiles hp ON c.current_household_profile_ref = hp.household_profile_record JOIN income_ranges ir ON hp.income_range_ref = ir.income_range_record WHERE a.state = {{string}} GROUP BY a.state, ir.lower_bound, ir.upper_bound ORDER BY clients DESC
history:addresses_clients_household_prof.template: addresses + clients + household_profiles + income_ranges pattern using addresses.address_record, addresses.state, clients.client_record, clients.current_address_ref

history:calendar_days_delivery_methods_f.sql: SELECT fc.warehouse_name, dm.carrier, sc.name AS support_center, count(DISTINCT mp.order_number) AS orders, sum(mp.net_paid_with_delivery) AS net_paid_with_delivery FROM mail_order_purchases mp JOIN fulfillment_centers fc ON mp.fulfillment_center_ref = fc.fulfillment_center_record JOIN delivery_methods dm ON mp.delivery_method_ref = dm.delivery_method_record JOIN support_centers sc ON mp.support_center_ref = sc.support_center_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON mp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} GROUP BY fc.warehouse_name, dm.carrier, sc.name ORDER BY net_paid_with_delivery DESC LIMIT {{number}}
history:calendar_days_delivery_methods_f.template: calendar_days + delivery_methods + fulfillment_centers + mail_order_purchases + support_centers pattern using calendar_days.calendar_day_record, calendar_days.year, delivery_methods.carrier, delivery_methods.delivery_method_record

history:calendar_days_fulfillment_center.sql: SELECT fc.warehouse_name, m.category, cd.year, cd.month_of_year, sum(sl.quantity_on_hand) AS stock_on_hand FROM stock_levels sl JOIN merchandise m ON sl.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON sl.calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN fulfillment_centers fc ON sl.fulfillment_center_ref = fc.fulfillment_center_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND cd.month_of_year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY fc.warehouse_name, m.category, cd.year, cd.month_of_year ORDER BY stock_on_hand ASC LIMIT {{number}}
history:calendar_days_fulfillment_center.template: calendar_days + fulfillment_centers + merchandise + stock_levels pattern using calendar_days.calendar_day_record, calendar_days.month_of_year, calendar_days.year, fulfillment_centers.fulfillment_center_record

history:calendar_days_mail_order_purchas.sql: WITH channel_customers AS ( SELECT {{string}} AS channel, op.billing_client_ref AS client_ref FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, mp.billing_client_ref AS client_ref FROM mail_order_purchases mp JOIN calendar_days cd ON mp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, sp.client_ref AS client_ref FROM store_purchases sp JOIN calendar_days cd ON sp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} ) SELECT channel, count(DISTINCT client_ref) AS customers FROM channel_customers WHERE client_ref IS NOT NULL GROUP BY channel ORDER BY customers DESC
history:calendar_days_mail_order_purchas.template: calendar_days + mail_order_purchases + online_purchases + store_purchases pattern using calendar_days.calendar_day_record, calendar_days.year, mail_order_purchases.billing_client_ref, mail_order_purchases.sale_calendar_day_ref

history:calendar_days_mail_order_refunds.sql: WITH refunds AS ( SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM online_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM mail_order_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, client_ref AS refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM store_refunds ) SELECT r.channel, m.category, count(DISTINCT r.refunded_client_ref) AS refunded_customers, sum(r.return_amount) AS refund_amount FROM refunds r JOIN merchandise m ON r.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON r.return_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY r.channel, m.category ORDER BY refund_amount DESC
history:calendar_days_mail_order_refunds.template: calendar_days + mail_order_refunds + merchandise + online_refunds + store_refunds pattern using calendar_days.calendar_day_record, calendar_days.year, merchandise.category, merchandise.merchandise_record

history:calendar_days_marketing_campaign.sql: SELECT mc.campaign_name, m.category, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN marketing_campaigns mc ON op.campaign_ref = mc.campaign_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY mc.campaign_name, m.category ORDER BY customers DESC LIMIT {{number}}
history:calendar_days_marketing_campaign.template: calendar_days + marketing_campaigns + merchandise + online_purchases pattern using calendar_days.calendar_day_record, calendar_days.year, marketing_campaigns.campaign_name, marketing_campaigns.campaign_record

history:calendar_days_merchandise_online.template: calendar_days + merchandise + online_purchases pattern using calendar_days.calendar_day_record, calendar_days.year, merchandise.category, merchandise.merchandise_record

history:calendar_days_merchandise_retail.sql: SELECT rl.state, rl.store_name, m.category, sum(sp.net_paid) AS net_paid, count(DISTINCT sp.client_ref) AS store_customers FROM store_purchases sp JOIN retail_locations rl ON sp.retail_location_ref = rl.retail_location_record JOIN merchandise m ON sp.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON sp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND rl.state = {{string}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY rl.state, rl.store_name, m.category ORDER BY net_paid DESC LIMIT {{number}}

assumptions: Client's current address and current demographic profile are used for state and gender.
Gender values are expected to be 'M' or 'F' as string literals.
A single value is expected for each of state, gender, and category filters.
No ordering or limit is applied; all matching group combinations are returned.
pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.canonical_question: How many online customers and how much net revenue came from a specific state, gender, and product category in a given year?

filters: client address state equals specified state
intent_signature.grain: one row per state, gender, product category, and year
intent_signature.measure: count of distinct online customers, sum of net paid

paraphrases: Show distinct online buyers and total net paid by state, gender, and merchandise category for a specific year.
What are the online sales and customer counts for a given state, customer gender, and product category in a specified year?
Break down online revenue and unique customers by state, gender segment, and category for a given year.
pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.sql_template: SELECT a.state, cp.gender, m.category, cd.year, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS online_customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN clients c ON op.billing_client_ref = c.client_record JOIN client_profiles cp ON c.current_client_profile_ref = cp.client_profile_record JOIN addresses a ON c.current_address_ref = a.address_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND a.state = {{string}} AND cp.gender = {{string}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY a.state, cp.gender, m.category, cd.year
pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.summary: Aggregates distinct online customer counts and total net paid segmented by customer state, gender, and product category for a specified year, joining purchases to client demographics and address data.

assumptions: The primary population is filtered by the customer's current address state, not the transaction address state.
Shipping and billing state customer counts use conditional distinct counts against separate state filter values, which may differ from the current address state filter.
A customer can appear in multiple counts (current, shipping, billing) if their addresses match the respective state filters.
The query joins billing client to the clients table; shipping client is counted separately via the shipping address join.
Left joins are used for shipping and billing addresses to retain all purchases even when address data is missing.
pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.canonical_question: How many unique customers from a given state made online purchases in a specific year, by shipping and billing state?

filters: Calendar year equals a specified year
Customer current address state equals a specified state
intent_signature.measure: Count of distinct customers (current address, shipping address, billing address)

paraphrases: For a given year, how many distinct online buyers currently live in a specific state, and how many shipped to or billed from that state?
What is the count of unique online customers by current address state, shipping state, and billing state for a particular year?
Show me the number of distinct online purchasers whose current address is in a given state, broken out by shipping and billing state matches, for a chosen year.
pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.sql_template: SELECT cd.year, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS current_state_customers, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN ship_a.state = {{string}} THEN op.shipping_client_ref END) AS shipping_state_customers, count(DISTINCT CASE WHEN bill_a.state = {{string}} THEN op.billing_client_ref END) AS billing_state_customers FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN clients c ON op.billing_client_ref = c.client_record JOIN addresses current_a ON c.current_address_ref = current_a.address_record LEFT JOIN addresses ship_a ON op.shipping_address_ref = ship_a.address_record LEFT JOIN addresses bill_a ON op.billing_address_ref = bill_a.address_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND current_a.state = {{string}} GROUP BY cd.year
pattern:history:addresses_calendar_days_.summary: Counts distinct online customers grouped by year, filtered to those whose current address is in a specified US state, and separately counts customers who shipped to or billed from another specified state during that year.

assumptions: Only the client's current address and current household profile are used.
Income bands are defined by lower_bound and upper_bound in the income_ranges table.
Results are ordered by customer count descending.
No time filter is applied; reflects current demographic snapshot.
pattern:history:addresses_clients_househ.canonical_question: How many customers in a given state fall into each household income band?

filters: client address state equals specified state
intent_signature.grain: one row per state and income band
intent_signature.time_window: none (point-in-time based on current address and household profile)

paraphrases: Show the distribution of customers by income range for a specific US state.
What is the customer count per income bracket for clients located in a given state?
Break down the number of distinct clients by household income band in a specified state.
pattern:history:addresses_clients_househ.sql_template: SELECT a.state, ir.lower_bound, ir.upper_bound, count(DISTINCT c.client_record) AS clients FROM clients c JOIN addresses a ON c.current_address_ref = a.address_record JOIN household_profiles hp ON c.current_household_profile_ref = hp.household_profile_record JOIN income_ranges ir ON hp.income_range_ref = ir.income_range_record WHERE a.state = {{string}} GROUP BY a.state, ir.lower_bound, ir.upper_bound ORDER BY clients DESC
pattern:history:addresses_clients_househ.summary: Counts distinct customers grouped by household income band for a specified US state, joining clients to their current address and household profile to retrieve income range boundaries.

assumptions: Order count is based on distinct order numbers, not line items, so multi-line orders are counted once.
Net paid with delivery includes delivery charges but excludes tax.
Results are ranked by net paid with delivery in descending order.
The number of rows returned is controlled by a configurable LIMIT value.
All joins (fulfillment center, delivery method, support center, calendar days) are inner joins, so purchases missing any of these references are excluded.
pattern:history:calendar_days_delivery_m.canonical_question: What are the top mail-order fulfillment centers and carriers by net paid revenue for a given year?

filters: Calendar year equals a specified year
intent_signature.grain: Fulfillment center × carrier × support center per year
intent_signature.measure: Count of distinct orders, sum of net paid with delivery

paraphrases: Which warehouse, carrier, and support center combinations generated the most net paid revenue from mail-order purchases in a specific year?
Show the top mail-order order counts and net paid amounts by fulfillment center, shipping carrier, and support center for a chosen year.
Rank mail-order sales by warehouse name, carrier, and support center based on net paid with delivery for a given calendar year.
pattern:history:calendar_days_delivery_m.sql_template: SELECT fc.warehouse_name, dm.carrier, sc.name AS support_center, count(DISTINCT mp.order_number) AS orders, sum(mp.net_paid_with_delivery) AS net_paid_with_delivery FROM mail_order_purchases mp JOIN fulfillment_centers fc ON mp.fulfillment_center_ref = fc.fulfillment_center_record JOIN delivery_methods dm ON mp.delivery_method_ref = dm.delivery_method_record JOIN support_centers sc ON mp.support_center_ref = sc.support_center_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON mp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} GROUP BY fc.warehouse_name, dm.carrier, sc.name ORDER BY net_paid_with_delivery DESC LIMIT {{number}}
pattern:history:calendar_days_delivery_m.summary: Aggregates mail-order purchases for a specified calendar year by fulfillment center, shipping carrier, and support center, returning the number of distinct orders and total net paid amount including delivery charges, ordered by net paid descending and limited to a specified number of rows.

assumptions: Stock levels are daily snapshots; summing across the month aggregates all daily records.
Results are ordered by stock on hand ascending (lowest first).
A LIMIT clause restricts the number of rows returned.
Filtering is applied on both year and month_of_year from the calendar_days dimension.
pattern:history:calendar_days_fulfillmen.canonical_question: Which fulfillment centers have the lowest stock on hand for a product category in a given year and month?

filters: month of year equals specified month
intent_signature.grain: one row per fulfillment center, product category, year, and month

paraphrases: Show total inventory by warehouse for a specific product category, year, and month, sorted by lowest stock first.
What are the bottom warehouses by quantity on hand for a given category and month?
List fulfillment centers with the least inventory for a product category in a specific month and year.
pattern:history:calendar_days_fulfillmen.sql_template: SELECT fc.warehouse_name, m.category, cd.year, cd.month_of_year, sum(sl.quantity_on_hand) AS stock_on_hand FROM stock_levels sl JOIN merchandise m ON sl.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON sl.calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN fulfillment_centers fc ON sl.fulfillment_center_ref = fc.fulfillment_center_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND cd.month_of_year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY fc.warehouse_name, m.category, cd.year, cd.month_of_year ORDER BY stock_on_hand ASC LIMIT {{number}}
pattern:history:calendar_days_fulfillmen.summary: Sums daily inventory quantity on hand by fulfillment center and product category for a specified year, month, and category, returning the lowest-stocked warehouses up to a specified row limit.

assumptions: Channel labels are provided as string literals in the query.
Customers with NULL client references are excluded.
The same year filter is applied independently to each channel.
Results are ordered by customer count descending.
pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.canonical_question: How many distinct customers purchased through each sales channel in a given year?
paraphrases: What is the unique customer count per channel (online, mail-order, store) for a specific year?
Show me the number of distinct buyers by sales channel for a given calendar year.
Which channel had the most customers in a particular year?
pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.sql_template: WITH channel_customers AS ( SELECT {{string}} AS channel, op.billing_client_ref AS client_ref FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, mp.billing_client_ref AS client_ref FROM mail_order_purchases mp JOIN calendar_days cd ON mp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, sp.client_ref AS client_ref FROM store_purchases sp JOIN calendar_days cd ON sp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} ) SELECT channel, count(DISTINCT client_ref) AS customers FROM channel_customers WHERE client_ref IS NOT NULL GROUP BY channel ORDER BY customers DESC
pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.summary: Counts distinct customers across online, mail-order, and store purchase channels for a specified calendar year, combining all three channels via UNION ALL and grouping by channel.

assumptions: Channel labels are provided as string literals.
All three refund tables (online_refunds, mail_order_refunds, store_refunds) are combined via UNION ALL.
Results are ordered by refund amount descending.
The store_refunds table uses 'client_ref' while the other tables use 'refunded_client_ref'.
pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.canonical_question: What are the total refund amounts and distinct refunded customers by channel and product category for a given year?

intent_signature.grain: one row per channel and product category
intent_signature.measure: sum of refund amount, count of distinct refunded customers

paraphrases: Show refund totals and unique refunded customer counts per sales channel and merchandise category for a specific year.
How much was refunded and how many customers received refunds by channel and category in a given year?
Break down refund amounts and refunded customer counts across online, mail-order, and store channels for a product category and year.
pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.sql_template: WITH refunds AS ( SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM online_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM mail_order_refunds UNION ALL SELECT {{string}} AS channel, client_ref AS refunded_client_ref, merchandise_ref, return_calendar_day_ref, return_amount FROM store_refunds ) SELECT r.channel, m.category, count(DISTINCT r.refunded_client_ref) AS refunded_customers, sum(r.return_amount) AS refund_amount FROM refunds r JOIN merchandise m ON r.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON r.return_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY r.channel, m.category ORDER BY refund_amount DESC
pattern:history:calendar_days_mail_order.summary: Aggregates refund amounts and distinct refunded customer counts across all three return channels (online, mail-order, store) for a specified product category and calendar year, grouped by channel and category.

assumptions: Only online purchases with a campaign reference are included.
Results are ordered by customer count descending.
A LIMIT clause restricts the number of rows returned.
A single merchandise category filter is applied.
pattern:history:calendar_days_marketing_.canonical_question: Which marketing campaigns drove the most online customers and revenue for a product category in a given year?

intent_signature.grain: one row per marketing campaign and product category
intent_signature.measure: count of distinct customers, sum of net paid

paraphrases: Show distinct online buyers and net paid by campaign and merchandise category for a specific year.
What are the top campaigns by customer count for a given product category and year?
Rank marketing campaigns by unique online customers for a specified category and calendar year.
pattern:history:calendar_days_marketing_.sql_template: SELECT mc.campaign_name, m.category, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN marketing_campaigns mc ON op.campaign_ref = mc.campaign_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY mc.campaign_name, m.category ORDER BY customers DESC LIMIT {{number}}
pattern:history:calendar_days_marketing_.summary: Counts distinct online customers and sums net paid by marketing campaign and product category for a specified year and category, returning the top campaigns by customer count up to a specified row limit.

assumptions: Profit margin is computed as sum(net_profit) / nullif(sum(net_paid), 0) to avoid division by zero.
Only the online channel is included; no cross-channel comparison.
A single category filter is applied; results return one row per year/category combination.
pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.canonical_question: What is the net revenue and profit margin for online sales by product category in a given year?

intent_signature.grain: one row per year and product category
intent_signature.measure: sum of net paid, sum of net profit, profit margin (net profit / net paid)

paraphrases: Show total net paid, net profit, and profit margin for online purchases by category for a specific year.
What are the online channel revenue and profitability metrics per product category in a given year?
How profitable were online sales for each merchandise category in a specified year?
pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.sql_template: SELECT cd.year, m.category, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid, sum(op.net_profit) AS net_profit, sum(op.net_profit) / nullif(sum(op.net_paid), {{number}}) AS profit_margin FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record JOIN merchandise m ON op.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY cd.year, m.category
pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.summary: Calculates total net paid, total net profit, and profit margin ratio for online purchases filtered to a specific product category and calendar year.

assumptions: Results are ordered by net paid descending.
A LIMIT clause restricts the number of rows returned.
State filter applies to the retail location's state, not the customer's address.
A single merchandise category filter is applied.
pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.canonical_question: Which stores in a given state had the highest net sales for a product category in a specific year?

filters: retail location state equals specified state
intent_signature.grain: one row per state, store name, and product category
intent_signature.measure: sum of net paid, count of distinct store customers

paraphrases: Show net paid and distinct customer counts by store and merchandise category for a given state and year.
What are the top retail locations by revenue for a specific product category, state, and year?
Rank stores in a given state by net sales for a particular merchandise category and calendar year.
pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.sql_template: SELECT rl.state, rl.store_name, m.category, sum(sp.net_paid) AS net_paid, count(DISTINCT sp.client_ref) AS store_customers FROM store_purchases sp JOIN retail_locations rl ON sp.retail_location_ref = rl.retail_location_record JOIN merchandise m ON sp.merchandise_ref = m.merchandise_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON sp.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} AND rl.state = {{string}} AND m.category = {{string}} GROUP BY rl.state, rl.store_name, m.category ORDER BY net_paid DESC LIMIT {{number}}
pattern:history:calendar_days_merchandis.summary: Sums net paid and counts distinct customers by retail store and product category for a specified state, category, and calendar year, returning the top stores by revenue up to a specified row limit.

assumptions: Results are ordered by net paid descending.
A LIMIT clause restricts the number of rows returned.
Page type comes from the site_pages table joined via site_page_ref.
Online property name comes from the online_properties table.
pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pro.canonical_question: Which online properties and page types generated the most revenue and customers in a given year?

intent_signature.grain: one row per online property and page type
intent_signature.measure: count of distinct customers, sum of net paid

paraphrases: Show net paid and distinct customer counts by website and page type for a specific year.
What are the top online properties by revenue and unique buyers, broken down by page type, for a given year?
Rank online properties and site page types by net revenue for a specified calendar year.
pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pro.sql_template: SELECT op2.name AS online_property, sp.page_type, count(DISTINCT op.billing_client_ref) AS customers, sum(op.net_paid) AS net_paid FROM online_purchases op JOIN online_properties op2 ON op.online_property_ref = op2.online_property_record JOIN site_pages sp ON op.site_page_ref = sp.site_page_record JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year = {{number}} GROUP BY op2.name, sp.page_type ORDER BY net_paid DESC LIMIT {{number}}
pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pro.summary: Aggregates distinct customer counts and total net paid by online property name and site page type for a specified calendar year, returning the top results by revenue up to a specified row limit.

assumptions: Retention is defined as having purchases in both specified years.
The HAVING clause checks that the customer appears in exactly the number of distinct years provided (2).
NULL billing client references are excluded.
No channel breakdown is returned; result is a single scalar count.
pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pur.canonical_question: How many online customers made purchases in both of two specified years?

filters: calendar year is one of two specified years
billing client reference is not null

paraphrases: What is the count of retained online buyers who purchased in two consecutive years?
How many online customers were active in both year A and year B?
Show the number of online shoppers who returned across two given years.
pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pur.sql_template: WITH customer_years AS ( SELECT op.billing_client_ref, cd.year FROM online_purchases op JOIN calendar_days cd ON op.sale_calendar_day_ref = cd.calendar_day_record WHERE cd.year IN ({{number}}, {{number}}) AND op.billing_client_ref IS NOT NULL GROUP BY op.billing_client_ref, cd.year ) SELECT count(*) AS retained_online_customers FROM ( SELECT billing_client_ref FROM customer_years GROUP BY billing_client_ref HAVING count(DISTINCT year) = {{number}} ) retained
pattern:history:calendar_days_online_pur.summary: Counts online customers who placed at least one purchase in each of two specified calendar years, measuring year-over-year retention for the online channel.
